verses.text: The adversaries offer to help build, claiming to seek the same God, but Zerubbabel refuses: 'ye have nothing to do with us.' He will not yoke the holy work to mixed and false worship. Discernment guards God's work from help that would corrupt it.

The adversaries offer to help build, claiming to seek the same God, but Zerubbabel refuses: 'ye have nothing to do with us.' He will not yoke the holy work to mixed and false worship. Discernment guards God's work from help that would corrupt it.

The adversaries offer to help build, claiming to seek the same God, but Zerubbabel refuses: 'ye have nothing to do with us.' He will not yoke the holy work to mixed and false worship. Discernment guards God's work from help that would corrupt it.

Rebuffed, the people of the land weaken the builders' hands and hire counsellors to frustrate the work for years. Opposition turns from offered friendship to open hindrance. Those who cannot join God's work will often try to stop it, wearing down the weak-handed by discouragement.

Rebuffed, the people of the land weaken the builders' hands and hire counsellors to frustrate the work for years. Opposition turns from offered friendship to open hindrance. Those who cannot join God's work will often try to stop it, wearing down the weak-handed by discouragement.

Letters of accusation are sent to successive Persian kings by the officials of the province. The enemies take their complaints to the highest power. The work of God seldom goes unopposed; its foes are diligent to enlist authority against it.

Letters of accusation are sent to successive Persian kings by the officials of the province. The enemies take their complaints to the highest power. The work of God seldom goes unopposed; its foes are diligent to enlist authority against it.

Their letter warns the king that if this rebellious city is rebuilt, its people will refuse tribute and taxes. They cloak their malice in concern for the king's revenue. Slander is most dangerous when it dresses self-interest as loyalty.

Their letter warns the king that if this rebellious city is rebuilt, its people will refuse tribute and taxes. They cloak their malice in concern for the king's revenue. Slander is most dangerous when it dresses self-interest as loyalty.

Their letter warns the king that if this rebellious city is rebuilt, its people will refuse tribute and taxes. They cloak their malice in concern for the king's revenue. Slander is most dangerous when it dresses self-interest as loyalty.

They claim that a finished Jerusalem will leave the king no portion beyond the river and prove hurtful to kings. The charge is exaggerated to alarm. Opponents of God's people will paint them as a threat to stir up fear and hostility.

They claim that a finished Jerusalem will leave the king no portion beyond the river and prove hurtful to kings. The charge is exaggerated to alarm. Opponents of God's people will paint them as a threat to stir up fear and hostility.

They claim that a finished Jerusalem will leave the king no portion beyond the river and prove hurtful to kings. The charge is exaggerated to alarm. Opponents of God's people will paint them as a threat to stir up fear and hostility.

The king replies that search has been made, and the city is found to have a record of rebellion against kings. A grain of truth is used to damn the whole. The past sins of God's people can be turned by enemies into weapons against their present good.

The king replies that search has been made, and the city is found to have a record of rebellion against kings. A grain of truth is used to damn the whole. The past sins of God's people can be turned by enemies into weapons against their present good.

The king replies that search has been made, and the city is found to have a record of rebellion against kings. A grain of truth is used to damn the whole. The past sins of God's people can be turned by enemies into weapons against their present good.

The king notes that mighty kings once ruled there and orders the work to cease, 'take heed now that ye fail not to do this.' The decree halts the building. Earthly power, swayed by false report, can stand for a time against the purposes of God.

The king notes that mighty kings once ruled there and orders the work to cease, 'take heed now that ye fail not to do this.' The decree halts the building. Earthly power, swayed by false report, can stand for a time against the purposes of God.

The king notes that mighty kings once ruled there and orders the work to cease, 'take heed now that ye fail not to do this.' The decree halts the building. Earthly power, swayed by false report, can stand for a time against the purposes of God.

Armed with the letter, the adversaries force the Jews to stop, and the work ceases until the second year of Darius. The house of God stands unfinished for years. God's work may be delayed by man's opposition, but the delay is in His hand, not theirs alone.
